name: accounts
description: Manage OAuth-authenticated third-party accounts (e.g. Google Calendar). Add a new account by emitting a one-tap consent URL via Telegram; list and disconnect existing accounts. The agent does NOT see refresh tokens or client secrets — only nicknames and provider names.

tools.name: connect_account
tools.description: Start an OAuth flow for a new account. Returns a one-time consent URL the user taps to authorize Xibi. The credential is persisted only after the user completes the consent screen — this tool does NOT itself save any token. YELLOW tier: creates a transient pending_state row.

nickname.description: Short name the user picks (e.g. 'afya', 'work', 'personal'). Must be unique per provider.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List configured OAuth accounts for this instance. GREEN tier — read-only.

tools.name: backfill_email_alias
tools.description: Populate metadata.email_alias for connected OAuth accounts that are missing it (legacy onboards from before step-108 auto-capture). Iterates accounts, refreshes each token, fetches Google userinfo, writes the verified email back. Idempotent. YELLOW tier: writes only the missing field, never touches secrets.

tools.name: disconnect_account
tools.description: Remove an OAuth account: deletes the metadata row, deletes the encrypted secret, and (best-effort) revokes the token at the provider. YELLOW tier — destructive but reversible (user can re-connect).

nickname.description: Nickname of the account to remove.

revoke_at_provider.description: If true (default), also POST to provider's revoke endpoint.
